name: Old Alresford
overview: Old Alresford is a village and civil parish in Hampshire, about 0.6 miles north of the Georgian town of New Alresford and roughly 7 miles north-east of Winchester, with a population of around 599. First mentioned in an Anglo-Saxon charter of 701 as 'Alresforda' (the ford by the alder tree), it has a village green with a stream and the Grade II* church of St Mary the Virgin, a brick building of the 1750s where the naval hero Admiral George Brydges Rodney is buried. Alresford Pond, created by a 12th-century Bishop of Winchester, lies between Old and New Alresford.
character: A tranquil, historic green-and-stream village with many listed buildings, set just across the water meadows from the amenities of New Alresford.
amenities: Village green with stream and pond nearby
St Mary the Virgin Church (Grade II*)
Numerous listed farmhouses and country houses
Full town amenities of New Alresford within ~1 mile (shops, pubs, cafes)
Watercress Line heritage railway at New Alresford
Walks to Alresford Pond and the Alre valley

schools.name: Primary schools in New Alresford (e.g. Sun Hill / The Perins catchment)

commutes.notes: Via Winchester or Alton mainline stations

prices.summary: A desirable village of period and listed houses beside the green; values are supported by scarcity, heritage and proximity to New Alresford and Winchester. See Rightmove for current sold-price detail.

thingsToDo: Walks to Alresford Pond and along the Alre valley
St Mary the Virgin Church and the Rodney tombs
Nearby Hinton Ampner House and Garden (National Trust)
placesToEat: Pubs, cafes and restaurants in adjoining New Alresford (e.g. Caracoli, The Globe on the Lake)
pros: Pretty village green and stream with strong heritage
Town amenities of New Alresford on the doorstep
Many listed buildings and a Grade II* church
cons: Few amenities within the village itself (relies on New Alresford)
No railway station; nearest mainline at Winchester/Alton
Limited supply of homes keeps prices high
whoItSuits: Buyers wanting a quiet, heritage-rich village green setting with the convenience of New Alresford a short stroll away.
